name: Brit Distortion
explanation: An amp-in-a-box British distortion. A tightening high-pass feeds a hot gain stage into a soft-knee/hard-rail clipper (the diode + op-amp behaviour of a cranked British amp), which then drives an interactive passive 3-band tone stack: a low shelf (Bass), a peaking bell around 650 Hz (Mid) and a high shelf (Treble). The bands load each other like a real passive network, so you can dial scooped 'crunch' or mid-forward 'roar'. Controls: Gain, Bass, Mid, Treble and Level.
